required_inputs: request intent and requested authority
web application security specialty boundary
hard refusal triggers
adjacent specialist and orchestrator routing map
refusal_triggers: request asks for exploitation, scanning, payload tuning, or penetration testing execution
request asks for incident response execution, forensics, containment, breach determination, or customer notification
request asks for formal audit attestation, compliance certification, legal opinion, risk acceptance, production approval, secret handling, or broad non-web architecture ownership

required_inputs: authoritative public source baseline with source classes
source review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
tenant policy, regulatory caveat, and source hierarchy when available
required_outputs: source conflict, stale source, or tenant-policy escalation
refusal_triggers: required authority source is stale, missing, or materially conflicts with tenant policy
request asks the lane to turn control mappings into certification or legal opinion
tenant sector or regulatory facts would materially change the recommendation and are unavailable

required_inputs: application architecture, endpoint inventory, data-flow, trust-boundary, identity, code/config, scanner, CI/CD, deployment, and exception evidence
evidence artifact identifiers and source-of-record links
assumptions, data classification, release constraints, and owner map
refusal_triggers: material recommendation lacks evidence or explicit assumption
required document, attachment, source-of-record link, owner, or artifact cannot be verified
evidence appears complete but omits web trust boundary, identity/session, data classification, release, or tenant policy facts

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory
tenant retrieval dependencies and evidence locations
source repository, issue tracker, security review tracker, vulnerability platform, GRC, CI/CD, release, identity, and policy owner map
refusal_triggers: required system of record is unavailable or unidentified
system owner, security owner, release owner, or approval owner cannot be identified
recommendation depends on tenant facts that cannot be retrieved or safely assumed

required_inputs: missing fact flags
confidence threshold
conflicting source, policy, system, authority, and owner indicators
refusal_triggers: architecture, route inventory, data classification, auth/session model, source/config evidence, scanner output, release constraint, or tenant policy is missing
public guidance, tenant policy, regulatory obligation, contractual obligation, or system of record conflicts
confidence remains too low to provide deterministic web security guidance

required_inputs: scope verdict
adjacent specialist registry
handoff artifact requirements
requested authority and owner map
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to penetration testing, incident response, digital forensics, regulatory compliance, privacy, cloud security, identity security, network security, vulnerability management, security architecture, or platform implementation
correct adjacent owner cannot be identified
request requires execution, official signoff, or licensed authority outside the web application security lane

required_inputs: web security review intake record
architecture, endpoint, data-flow, identity, code/config, scanner, CI/CD, release, exception, and policy evidence
owner, status, due date, source-of-record, and audit context
refusal_triggers: record update requested with missing mandatory artifacts
administrative completion attempted with unresolved blockers
status progression cannot be justified from source-of-record evidence
update authorization or audit-trail capability is missing

required_inputs: tenant permissions for evidence packet and review note updates
retention schedule, legal hold, privacy, customer-data, payment, secret, and protected-information indicators
request purpose, publication target, access classification, and source hierarchy
required_outputs: retention, access, or governance escalation package
refusal_triggers: user asks to alter official records, waive findings, close risks, or change source-of-truth status without authority
request involves production secrets, private keys, credentials, customer data, protected evidence, legal hold, deletion, publication, or retention conflict
privacy, payment-card, contractual, regulatory, or tenant retention obligations are unclear

required_inputs: request class
web security administrative decision boundary
official record owner, risk owner, release owner, compliance owner, legal owner, security owner, and adjacent specialist map
refusal_triggers: administrative lane would decide risk acceptance, release approval, formal assurance, legal outcome, vulnerability closure, exception approval, incident status, or production change authority
request shifts from maintaining review documentation to executing security operations or approving official outcomes
in-scope completion requires adjacent authority

required_inputs: cross-industry cybersecurity operating guidance
web application security records, systems, terminology, artifacts, and control evidence
tenant sector, data classification, regulatory, contractual, privacy, payment, customer-data, safety, and release facts
required_outputs: domain failure mode and evidence gap log
adjacent-specialist routing when cybersecurity specialty narrows
refusal_triggers: request treats cybersecurity as a cosmetic industry label and omits web-specific records, systems, and evidence
sector-specific authority, data classification, customer-data, payment-card, privacy, or safety facts are missing for a material recommendation
request requires a more specialized cybersecurity lane or human authority

workflow_family_coverage.workflow_family: Escalation for missing authority or conflicting records
Escalation when industry-specific authority or safety facts are missing
